accepted_evidence: Six project-owned 2048-pixel texture candidates import reproducibly with recorded hashes and no external vendored source data.
The isolated UE 5.8 command creates explicit bark and two-sided foliage materials plus separate trunk and foliage static meshes.
Both static meshes have positive bounds and Nanite PreserveArea enabled.
The adult prototype has a readable straight bole, open horizontal branch structure, and a continuous crown silhouette at the 60 m and 150 m proxy distances.
All five required 1280x720 review views were captured from the reproducible isolated review map.
rejection_reasons: The 225-283 cm crossed foliage cards remain visibly flat and intersecting at close range instead of resolving as small coigue leaf sprays.
Hard card and atlas-cutout silhouettes form dark hanging sheets under the crown and remain visible in both turntable views.
The bark material renders too dark for its vertically fissured texture to remain readable at guide-seat distance.
The single adult form is too regular and top-heavy to establish a lifelike native forest band or production-ready species library.
Wind response, age and crown variants, forest strata, corridor ecology, guide review, and measured desktop or VR performance remain unproven.

review_contract.capture_redistribution: not approved; hashes and reproducible paths only are retained in the open-source repository

next_iteration_requirements: Replace metre-scale crossed cards with denser 0.55-1.10 m twig sprays whose planes follow branch direction and break up before the closeup gate.
Expose enough bark albedo and neutral fill for the fissured surface to remain readable without flattening branch self-shadow.
Add wind parameters and at least adult open-grown, adult forest-grown, and intermediate crown variants before corridor placement.
Repeat the same five-view isolated gate and require closeup acceptance before any bounded corridor comparison.
